steps.agent_message:
Based on your answers, here are my **top 3 template recommendations**:


**1. ⛊ Vulnerability Posture** (score: 25)
Dedicated vuln management view. Patch SLA KPIs + CVE list with severity + detail with CISA KEV, CVSS, blast radius + remediation chat.
Match reasons: strip_cell_count_match

**2. ◐ Executive Risk Summary** (score: 22)
CISO/board-level single-page view. Wide KPI strip (8 cells) + 2-panel with risk heatmap + framework posture cards. No chat, no drill-down — pure signal.
Match reasons: strip_cell_count_match

**3. ⊛ Access Review & Certification** (score: 21)
IAM-focused: access reviews, orphaned accounts, privilege creep. Links Workday terminations → Cornerstone certs → Security access data.
Match reasons: strip_cell_count_match


**Select a template** by name or number, or tell me if you want something different.

recommended.description: Dedicated vuln management view. Patch SLA KPIs + CVE list with severity + detail with CISA KEV, CVSS, blast radius + remediation chat.

recommended.name: Executive Risk Summary
recommended.description: CISO/board-level single-page view. Wide KPI strip (8 cells) + 2-panel with risk heatmap + framework posture cards. No chat, no drill-down — pure signal.

recommended.name: Access Review & Certification
recommended.description: IAM-focused: access reviews, orphaned accounts, privilege creep. Links Workday terminations → Cornerstone certs → Security access data.

**Structure:** topbar → posture_strip → three_panel

**AI Chat:** Yes
**Causal Graph:** No

strip_kpis.display_name: Vulnerabilities by Asset Criticality - by Host
Vulnerabilities by Asset Criticality - by OS

focus_areas: alert queues and triage lists with drill-down to investigation context
incident timelines, evidence, root cause and remediation status
vulnerability and CVE correlation to assets and exposure scoring
operational SLAs and responder workload / capacity dashboards

metric_recommendations.name: Vulnerabilities by Asset Criticality - by Host
metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: How many critical vulnerabilities exist on our most critical assets, and how has that changed?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter records to those where severity is CRITICAL and status indicates the vulnerability is open (use the status column)
Join to the qualys_hosts table on qualys_hosts.id = qualys_host_detections.host_id to bring in host attributes (ip, os)
Group by qualys_host_detections.host_id and the detection date (derive a daily snapshot from last_found_datetime) to produce a daily count of critical open vulnerabilities per host
Produce a 30-day rolling series and compare the latest 30-day total to the prior 30-day total to show trend direction
metric_recommendations.implementation_note: Requires daily snapshots derived from qualys_host_detections.last_found_datetime; gold table gold_vulnerabilities_weekly_by_host recommended for reporting

metric_recommendations.name: Critical vuln count (per host)
metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What is our Critical vuln count (per host)?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter records to those where severity is CRITICAL and status indicates the vulnerability is open (use the status column)
Join to the qualys_hosts table on qualys_hosts.id = qualys_host_detections.host_id to bring in host attributes (ip, os)
Group by qualys_host_detections.host_id and the detection date (derive a daily snapshot from last_found_datetime) to produce a daily count of critical open vulnerabilities per host
Produce a 30-day rolling series and compare the latest 30-day total to the prior 30-day total to show trend direction
metric_recommendations.implementation_note: Requires daily snapshots derived from qualys_host_detections.last_found_datetime; gold table gold_vulnerabilities_weekly_by_host recommended for reporting

metric_recommendations.name: 30-day trend of critical vulnerabilities
metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What is our 30-day trend of critical vulnerabilities?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter records to those where severity is CRITICAL and status indicates the vulnerability is open (use the status column)
Join to the qualys_hosts table on qualys_hosts.id = qualys_host_detections.host_id to bring in host attributes (ip, os)
Group by qualys_host_detections.host_id and the detection date (derive a daily snapshot from last_found_datetime) to produce a daily count of critical open vulnerabilities per host
Produce a 30-day rolling series and compare the latest 30-day total to the prior 30-day total to show trend direction
metric_recommendations.implementation_note: Requires daily snapshots derived from qualys_host_detections.last_found_datetime; gold table gold_vulnerabilities_weekly_by_host recommended for reporting

metric_recommendations.name: Vulnerabilities by Asset Criticality - by OS
metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: How many critical vulnerabilities exist on our most critical assets, and how has that changed?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter to records where severity is CRITICAL and status indicates open
Join to qualys_hosts on qualys_hosts.id = qualys_host_detections.host_id to obtain qualys_hosts.os
Group by qualys_hosts.os and the detection snapshot date (daily) to produce a daily count per OS
Aggregate daily series to weekly for the gold table and compare week-over-week to surface trends
metric_recommendations.implementation_note: OS-level grouping requires join to qualys_hosts; recommended gold table gold_vulnerabilities_weekly_by_os

metric_recommendations.name: Critical vuln count by OS
metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What is our Critical vuln count by OS?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter to records where severity is CRITICAL and status indicates open
Join to qualys_hosts on qualys_hosts.id = qualys_host_detections.host_id to obtain qualys_hosts.os
Group by qualys_hosts.os and the detection snapshot date (daily) to produce a daily count per OS
Aggregate daily series to weekly for the gold table and compare week-over-week to surface trends
metric_recommendations.implementation_note: OS-level grouping requires join to qualys_hosts; recommended gold table gold_vulnerabilities_weekly_by_os

metric_recommendations.name: Weekly trend by OS
metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What is our Weekly trend by OS?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter to records where severity is CRITICAL and status indicates open
Join to qualys_hosts on qualys_hosts.id = qualys_host_detections.host_id to obtain qualys_hosts.os
Group by qualys_hosts.os and the detection snapshot date (daily) to produce a daily count per OS
Aggregate daily series to weekly for the gold table and compare week-over-week to surface trends
metric_recommendations.implementation_note: OS-level grouping requires join to qualys_hosts; recommended gold table gold_vulnerabilities_weekly_by_os

metric_recommendations.name: Vulnerability Fix Backlog by Host
metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: Which teams have the largest backlog of unresolved vulnerabilities and how has each team's backlog trended over the last 90 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter to records where status indicates the vulnerability is still open
For each host_id, compute days since first observed using last_found_datetime and produce a daily snapshot row per open finding
Group by host_id and snapshot date to produce daily open vulnerability counts per host and compute average days open per host over the period
Roll up daily snapshots into weekly aggregates for backlog reporting in the gold table
metric_recommendations.implementation_note: Team-level assignment requires additional host-to-team mapping outside current tables; current recommendation produces host-level backlog as a proxy

metric_recommendations.name: Open vulnerability count (per host)
metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What is our Open vulnerability count (per host)?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter to records where status indicates the vulnerability is still open
For each host_id, compute days since first observed using last_found_datetime and produce a daily snapshot row per open finding
Group by host_id and snapshot date to produce daily open vulnerability counts per host and compute average days open per host over the period
Roll up daily snapshots into weekly aggregates for backlog reporting in the gold table
metric_recommendations.implementation_note: Team-level assignment requires additional host-to-team mapping outside current tables; current recommendation produces host-level backlog as a proxy

gold_model_sql.name: gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily_snapshot
gold_model_sql.sql_query:
{{ config(materialized='incremental', unique_key='connection_id, snapshot_date, host_id, qid', incremental_strategy='merge', on_schema_change='append_new_columns') }}

WITH source_data AS (
  SELECT
    hd.connection_id AS connection_id,
    date_trunc('day', CAST(hd.last_found_datetime AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE)) AS snapshot_date,
    hd.host_id AS host_id,
    h.ip AS ip,
    h.os AS os,
    hd.severity AS severity,
    CASE
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT)) IN ('5','critical','crit') THEN 'critical'
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT)) IN ('4','high') THEN 'high'
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT)) IN ('3','medium','med') THEN 'medium'
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT)) IN ('2','low') THEN 'low'
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT)) IN ('1','info','informational') THEN 'info'
      ELSE lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT))
    END AS severity_label,
    CASE
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT)) IN ('5','critical','crit','4','high') THEN true
      ELSE false
    END AS is_critical,
    hd.status AS status,
    CASE
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.status AS TEXT)) IN ('open','opened') THEN true
      ELSE false
    END AS is_open,
    CAST(hd.last_found_datetime AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) AS last_found_datetime,
    CAST( (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - CAST(hd.last_found_datetime AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE)) / INTERVAL '1 day' AS INTEGER) AS days_open,
    hd.qid AS qid,
    app.name AS application_name,
    app.version AS application_version
  FROM
    {{ source('silver','qualys_host_detections') }} AS hd
  JOIN
    {{ source('silver','qualys_hosts') }} AS h
    ON hd.host_id = h.id
    AND hd.connection_id = h.connection_id
  LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('silver','qualys_applications') }} AS app
    ON hd.host_id = app.host_id
    AND hd.connection_id = app.connection_id
  WHERE
    hd.connection_id = {{ var('connection_id') }}
    {% if is_incremental() %}
      AND CAST(hd.last_found_datetime AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) >= (CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 day')
    {% endif %}
)

SELECT
  sd.connection_id AS connection_id,
  sd.snapshot_date AS snapshot_date,
  sd.host_id AS host_id,
  sd.ip AS ip,
  sd.os AS os,
  sd.severity AS severity,
  sd.severity_label AS severity_label,
  sd.is_critical AS is_critical,
  sd.status AS status,
  sd.is_open AS is_open,
  sd.last_found_datetime AS last_found_datetime,
  sd.days_open AS days_open,
  sd.qid AS qid,
  sd.application_name AS application_name,
  sd.application_version AS application_version
FROM source_data AS sd
GROUP BY
  sd.connection_id,
  sd.snapshot_date,
  sd.host_id,
  sd.ip,
  sd.os,
  sd.severity,
  sd.severity_label,
  sd.is_critical,
  sd.status,
  sd.is_open,
  sd.last_found_datetime,
  sd.days_open,
  sd.qid,
  sd.application_name,
  sd.application_version;
gold_model_sql.description: Daily snapshot of Qualys vulnerabilities at host level, joining detections with host metadata and optional application context. Produces one row per (connection_id, snapshot_date, host_id, qid) with derived flags and timing metrics.

gold_model_sql.name: gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_weekly_snapshot
gold_model_sql.sql_query:
{{ config(materialized='incremental', unique_key='connection_id,week_start,host_id', incremental_strategy='merge', on_schema_change='append_new_columns') }}

WITH source_data AS (
  SELECT
    d.connection_id AS connection_id,
    DATE_TRUNC('week', CAST(d.snapshot_date AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE)) AS week_start,
    d.host_id AS host_id,
    d.ip AS ip,
    d.os AS os,
    d.is_critical AS is_critical,
    d.is_open AS is_open,
    d.days_open AS days_open
  FROM {{ ref('gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily_snapshot') }} AS d
  WHERE d.connection_id = {{ var('connection_id') }}
  {% if is_incremental() %}
    AND DATE_TRUNC('week', CAST(d.snapshot_date AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE)) >= (
      SELECT COALESCE(MAX(week_start), '1970-01-01'::timestamp AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')
      FROM {{ this }}
      WHERE connection_id = {{ var('connection_id') }}
    )
  {% endif %}
)

SELECT
  sd.connection_id AS connection_id,
  sd.week_start AS week_start,
  sd.host_id AS host_id,
  sd.ip AS ip,
  sd.os AS os,
  SUM(CASE WHEN sd.is_critical = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS critical_vuln_count_week,
  SUM(CASE WHEN sd.is_open = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS open_vuln_count_week,
  AVG(sd.days_open) AS avg_days_open_week
FROM source_data sd
GROUP BY
  sd.connection_id,
  sd.week_start,
  sd.host_id,
  sd.ip,
  sd.os
gold_model_sql.description: Weekly aggregation of Qualys vulnerability snapshots derived from daily gold model. Produces per-host weekly counts for critical and open vulnerabilities and the average days open.

gold_model_sql.name: gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_monthly_snapshot
gold_model_sql.sql_query:
{{ config(materialized='table', unique_key='connection_id, month_start, host_id, os', incremental_strategy='merge', on_schema_change='append_new_columns') }}

WITH monthly AS (
  SELECT
    src.connection_id AS connection_id,
    CAST(DATE_TRUNC('month', src.week_start) AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) AS month_start,
    src.host_id AS host_id,
    src.os AS os,
    SUM(src.critical_vuln_count_week) AS critical_vuln_count_month,
    SUM(src.open_vuln_count_week) AS open_vuln_count_month,
    CASE
      WHEN SUM(src.open_vuln_count_week) = 0 THEN NULL
      ELSE SUM(src.avg_days_open_week * src.open_vuln_count_week) / NULLIF(SUM(src.open_vuln_count_week), 0)
    END AS avg_days_open_month
  FROM {{ ref('gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_weekly_snapshot') }} AS src
  {% if is_incremental() %}
    WHERE src.week_start >= (
      SELECT COALESCE(MAX(month_start), '1970-01-01'::timestamp with time zone)
      FROM {{ this }}
    )
    AND src.connection_id = {{ var('connection_id') }}
  {% else %}
    WHERE src.connection_id = {{ var('connection_id') }}
  {% endif %}
  GROUP BY
    src.connection_id,
    CAST(DATE_TRUNC('month', src.week_start) AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE),
    src.host_id,
    src.os
)

SELECT
  m.connection_id,
  m.month_start,
  m.host_id,
  m.os,
  m.critical_vuln_count_month,
  m.open_vuln_count_month,
  m.avg_days_open_month
FROM monthly AS m;
gold_model_sql.description: Monthly aggregation of Qualys weekly vulnerability snapshots. Produces per-connection, per-host, per-OS monthly counts of critical and open vulnerabilities and a weighted average of days open (weighted by open_vuln_count_week). Uses ref('gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_weekly_snapshot') as the source and includes connection_id filtering. Materialized as a stable table for monthly reporting.

gold_model_sql.name: gold_snyk_issues_daily_snapshot
gold_model_sql.sql_query:
{{ config(materialized='incremental', unique_key='connection_id,organization_id,issue_package,publication_time', incremental_strategy='merge', on_schema_change='append_new_columns') }}

WITH source_data AS (
    SELECT
        src."connection_id" AS connection_id,
        src."organization_id" AS organization_id,
        -- normalize publication time to day-level snapshot (timestamp with time zone)
        CAST(date_trunc('day', CAST(src."v1.issue.publicationTime" AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE)) AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) AS snapshot_date,
        src."v1.issue.package" AS issue_package,
        src."v1.issue.language" AS issue_language,
        -- cast CVSS score to numeric when it is a numeric-looking string
        CASE
            WHEN src."v1.issue.cvssScore" ~ '^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN CAST(src."v1.issue.cvssScore" AS NUMERIC)
            ELSE NULL
        END AS cvss_score,
        -- derive high CVSS flag
        CASE
            WHEN src."v1.issue.cvssScore" ~ '^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' AND CAST(src."v1.issue.cvssScore" AS NUMERIC) >= 7.0 THEN TRUE
            ELSE FALSE
        END AS is_high_cvss,
        CAST(src."v1.issue.publicationTime" AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) AS publication_time,
        src."v1.issue.semver.vulnerable" AS vulnerable_semver_ranges
    FROM {{ source('silver', 'snyk_issues') }} AS src
    {% if is_incremental() %}
    WHERE src."connection_id" = '{{ var("connection_id") }}'
    {% else %}
    WHERE src."connection_id" = '{{ var("connection_id") }}'
    {% endif %}
)

SELECT
    sd.connection_id,
    sd.organization_id,
    sd.snapshot_date,
    sd.issue_package,
    sd.issue_language,
    sd.cvss_score,
    sd.is_high_cvss,
    sd.publication_time,
    sd.vulnerable_semver_ranges
FROM source_data AS sd
gold_model_sql.description: Daily snapshot of Snyk vulnerabilities (gold layer). Normalizes nested fields from the silver.snyk_issues source, casts CVSS to numeric, computes high-CVSS flag, and normalizes publication time to a daily snapshot_date. Incremental materialization filtered by connection_id.

gold_model_sql.name: gold_snyk_issues_weekly_snapshot
gold_model_sql.sql_query:
{{ config(materialized='incremental', unique_key=['connection_id','week_start','issue_language','issue_package'], incremental_strategy='merge', on_schema_change='append_new_columns') }}

select
  daily.connection_id,
  CAST(DATE_TRUNC('week', daily.snapshot_date) AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) AS week_start,
  daily.issue_language,
  daily.issue_package,
  SUM(CASE WHEN daily.is_high_cvss = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS count_high_cvss_week,
  json_build_object(
    'min', MIN(daily.cvss_score),
    'p25', percentile_disc(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY daily.cvss_score),
    'p50', percentile_disc(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY daily.cvss_score),
    'p75', percentile_disc(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY daily.cvss_score),
    'max', MAX(daily.cvss_score),
    'avg', AVG(daily.cvss_score)
  ) AS cvss_distribution_week
from {{ ref('gold_snyk_issues_daily_snapshot') }} as daily
where daily.connection_id = {{ var('connection_id') }}
{% if is_incremental() %}
  -- incremental runs only process recent snapshots (by snapshot_date -> week_start)
  AND daily.snapshot_date >= (
    select coalesce(max(week_start), '1970-01-01'::timestamp) from {{ this }}
  )
{% endif %}
group by
  daily.connection_id,
  CAST(DATE_TRUNC('week', daily.snapshot_date) AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE),
  daily.issue_language,
  daily.issue_package;
gold_model_sql.description: Weekly aggregation of Snyk issue snapshots (vulnerabilities). Computes week_start from daily snapshot_date, counts high-CVSS issues per week and returns a CVSS distribution (min, p25, p50, p75, max, avg) grouped by connection, language, and package.

messages.content:
I'm the **Layout Advisor**. I see you have **metric recommendations** and **gold model SQL** from the upstream pipeline.

• **8** metrics recommended
• **5** gold tables available

I'll recommend a layout and bind metrics to gold tables for human approval. Target output: **echarts**.

**Dashboard goals:** Vulnerability management dashboard
**Summary persona:** security_ops

Based on your answers, here are my **top 3 template recommendations**:


**1. ⛊ Vulnerability Posture** (score: 25)
Dedicated vuln management view. Patch SLA KPIs + CVE list with severity + detail with CISA KEV, CVSS, blast radius + remediation chat.
Match reasons: strip_cell_count_match

**2. ◐ Executive Risk Summary** (score: 22)
CISO/board-level single-page view. Wide KPI strip (8 cells) + 2-panel with risk heatmap + framework posture cards. No chat, no drill-down — pure signal.
Match reasons: strip_cell_count_match

**3. ⊛ Access Review & Certification** (score: 21)
IAM-focused: access reviews, orphaned accounts, privilege creep. Links Workday terminations → Cornerstone certs → Security access data.
Match reasons: strip_cell_count_match


**Select a template** by name or number, or tell me if you want something different.

Excellent choice — **Vulnerability Posture**.

**Structure:** topbar → posture_strip → three_panel
**Panels:** left: filter_bar + cve_cards | center: detail_sections + signals + asset_list | right: chat_panel
**KPI Strip:** 6 cells — Critical CVEs, Patch SLA Breach, CISA KEV Open…
**AI Chat:** Yes
**Causal Graph:** No

Would you like to **customize** anything (KPI labels, filters, theme, panel contents), or say **"looks good"** to generate the final spec?

Layout spec **finalized** ✓

→ Template: **Vulnerability Posture** (⛊)
→ Theme: dark
→ 3 KPI strip cells | 5 charts
→ Metric catalog enrichment: 8/8 metrics

The spec is ready for the downstream renderer.

metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What is our Weekly trend by OS?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter to records where severity is CRITICAL and status indicates open
Join to qualys_hosts on qualys_hosts.id = qualys_host_detections.host_id to obtain qualys_hosts.os
Group by qualys_hosts.os and the detection snapshot date (daily) to produce a daily count per OS
Aggregate daily series to weekly for the gold table and compare week-over-week to surface trends
metric_recommendations.implementation_note: OS-level grouping requires join to qualys_hosts; recommended gold table gold_vulnerabilities_weekly_by_os

metric_recommendations.name: Vulnerability Fix Backlog by Host
metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: Which teams have the largest backlog of unresolved vulnerabilities and how has each team's backlog trended over the last 90 days?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter to records where status indicates the vulnerability is still open
For each host_id, compute days since first observed using last_found_datetime and produce a daily snapshot row per open finding
Group by host_id and snapshot date to produce daily open vulnerability counts per host and compute average days open per host over the period
Roll up daily snapshots into weekly aggregates for backlog reporting in the gold table
metric_recommendations.implementation_note: Team-level assignment requires additional host-to-team mapping outside current tables; current recommendation produces host-level backlog as a proxy

metric_recommendations.name: Open vulnerability count (per host)
metric_recommendations.natural_language_question: What is our Open vulnerability count (per host)?
calculation_plan_steps: Start from the qualys_host_detections table
Filter to records where status indicates the vulnerability is still open
For each host_id, compute days since first observed using last_found_datetime and produce a daily snapshot row per open finding
Group by host_id and snapshot date to produce daily open vulnerability counts per host and compute average days open per host over the period
Roll up daily snapshots into weekly aggregates for backlog reporting in the gold table
metric_recommendations.implementation_note: Team-level assignment requires additional host-to-team mapping outside current tables; current recommendation produces host-level backlog as a proxy

gold_model_sql.name: gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily_snapshot
gold_model_sql.sql_query:
{{ config(materialized='incremental', unique_key='connection_id, snapshot_date, host_id, qid', incremental_strategy='merge', on_schema_change='append_new_columns') }}

WITH source_data AS (
  SELECT
    hd.connection_id AS connection_id,
    date_trunc('day', CAST(hd.last_found_datetime AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE)) AS snapshot_date,
    hd.host_id AS host_id,
    h.ip AS ip,
    h.os AS os,
    hd.severity AS severity,
    CASE
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT)) IN ('5','critical','crit') THEN 'critical'
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT)) IN ('4','high') THEN 'high'
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT)) IN ('3','medium','med') THEN 'medium'
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT)) IN ('2','low') THEN 'low'
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT)) IN ('1','info','informational') THEN 'info'
      ELSE lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT))
    END AS severity_label,
    CASE
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.severity AS TEXT)) IN ('5','critical','crit','4','high') THEN true
      ELSE false
    END AS is_critical,
    hd.status AS status,
    CASE
      WHEN lower(CAST(hd.status AS TEXT)) IN ('open','opened') THEN true
      ELSE false
    END AS is_open,
    CAST(hd.last_found_datetime AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) AS last_found_datetime,
    CAST( (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - CAST(hd.last_found_datetime AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE)) / INTERVAL '1 day' AS INTEGER) AS days_open,
    hd.qid AS qid,
    app.name AS application_name,
    app.version AS application_version
  FROM
    {{ source('silver','qualys_host_detections') }} AS hd
  JOIN
    {{ source('silver','qualys_hosts') }} AS h
    ON hd.host_id = h.id
    AND hd.connection_id = h.connection_id
  LEFT JOIN
    {{ source('silver','qualys_applications') }} AS app
    ON hd.host_id = app.host_id
    AND hd.connection_id = app.connection_id
  WHERE
    hd.connection_id = {{ var('connection_id') }}
    {% if is_incremental() %}
      AND CAST(hd.last_found_datetime AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) >= (CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 day')
    {% endif %}
)

SELECT
  sd.connection_id AS connection_id,
  sd.snapshot_date AS snapshot_date,
  sd.host_id AS host_id,
  sd.ip AS ip,
  sd.os AS os,
  sd.severity AS severity,
  sd.severity_label AS severity_label,
  sd.is_critical AS is_critical,
  sd.status AS status,
  sd.is_open AS is_open,
  sd.last_found_datetime AS last_found_datetime,
  sd.days_open AS days_open,
  sd.qid AS qid,
  sd.application_name AS application_name,
  sd.application_version AS application_version
FROM source_data AS sd
GROUP BY
  sd.connection_id,
  sd.snapshot_date,
  sd.host_id,
  sd.ip,
  sd.os,
  sd.severity,
  sd.severity_label,
  sd.is_critical,
  sd.status,
  sd.is_open,
  sd.last_found_datetime,
  sd.days_open,
  sd.qid,
  sd.application_name,
  sd.application_version;
gold_model_sql.description: Daily snapshot of Qualys vulnerabilities at host level, joining detections with host metadata and optional application context. Produces one row per (connection_id, snapshot_date, host_id, qid) with derived flags and timing metrics.

gold_model_sql.name: gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_weekly_snapshot
gold_model_sql.sql_query:
{{ config(materialized='incremental', unique_key='connection_id,week_start,host_id', incremental_strategy='merge', on_schema_change='append_new_columns') }}

WITH source_data AS (
  SELECT
    d.connection_id AS connection_id,
    DATE_TRUNC('week', CAST(d.snapshot_date AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE)) AS week_start,
    d.host_id AS host_id,
    d.ip AS ip,
    d.os AS os,
    d.is_critical AS is_critical,
    d.is_open AS is_open,
    d.days_open AS days_open
  FROM {{ ref('gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily_snapshot') }} AS d
  WHERE d.connection_id = {{ var('connection_id') }}
  {% if is_incremental() %}
    AND DATE_TRUNC('week', CAST(d.snapshot_date AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE)) >= (
      SELECT COALESCE(MAX(week_start), '1970-01-01'::timestamp AT TIME ZONE 'UTC')
      FROM {{ this }}
      WHERE connection_id = {{ var('connection_id') }}
    )
  {% endif %}
)

SELECT
  sd.connection_id AS connection_id,
  sd.week_start AS week_start,
  sd.host_id AS host_id,
  sd.ip AS ip,
  sd.os AS os,
  SUM(CASE WHEN sd.is_critical = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS critical_vuln_count_week,
  SUM(CASE WHEN sd.is_open = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS open_vuln_count_week,
  AVG(sd.days_open) AS avg_days_open_week
FROM source_data sd
GROUP BY
  sd.connection_id,
  sd.week_start,
  sd.host_id,
  sd.ip,
  sd.os
gold_model_sql.description: Weekly aggregation of Qualys vulnerability snapshots derived from daily gold model. Produces per-host weekly counts for critical and open vulnerabilities and the average days open.

gold_model_sql.name: gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_monthly_snapshot
gold_model_sql.sql_query:
{{ config(materialized='table', unique_key='connection_id, month_start, host_id, os', incremental_strategy='merge', on_schema_change='append_new_columns') }}

WITH monthly AS (
  SELECT
    src.connection_id AS connection_id,
    CAST(DATE_TRUNC('month', src.week_start) AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) AS month_start,
    src.host_id AS host_id,
    src.os AS os,
    SUM(src.critical_vuln_count_week) AS critical_vuln_count_month,
    SUM(src.open_vuln_count_week) AS open_vuln_count_month,
    CASE
      WHEN SUM(src.open_vuln_count_week) = 0 THEN NULL
      ELSE SUM(src.avg_days_open_week * src.open_vuln_count_week) / NULLIF(SUM(src.open_vuln_count_week), 0)
    END AS avg_days_open_month
  FROM {{ ref('gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_weekly_snapshot') }} AS src
  {% if is_incremental() %}
    WHERE src.week_start >= (
      SELECT COALESCE(MAX(month_start), '1970-01-01'::timestamp with time zone)
      FROM {{ this }}
    )
    AND src.connection_id = {{ var('connection_id') }}
  {% else %}
    WHERE src.connection_id = {{ var('connection_id') }}
  {% endif %}
  GROUP BY
    src.connection_id,
    CAST(DATE_TRUNC('month', src.week_start) AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE),
    src.host_id,
    src.os
)

SELECT
  m.connection_id,
  m.month_start,
  m.host_id,
  m.os,
  m.critical_vuln_count_month,
  m.open_vuln_count_month,
  m.avg_days_open_month
FROM monthly AS m;
gold_model_sql.description: Monthly aggregation of Qualys weekly vulnerability snapshots. Produces per-connection, per-host, per-OS monthly counts of critical and open vulnerabilities and a weighted average of days open (weighted by open_vuln_count_week). Uses ref('gold_qualys_vulnerabilities_weekly_snapshot') as the source and includes connection_id filtering. Materialized as a stable table for monthly reporting.

gold_model_sql.name: gold_snyk_issues_daily_snapshot
gold_model_sql.sql_query:
{{ config(materialized='incremental', unique_key='connection_id,organization_id,issue_package,publication_time', incremental_strategy='merge', on_schema_change='append_new_columns') }}

WITH source_data AS (
    SELECT
        src."connection_id" AS connection_id,
        src."organization_id" AS organization_id,
        -- normalize publication time to day-level snapshot (timestamp with time zone)
        CAST(date_trunc('day', CAST(src."v1.issue.publicationTime" AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE)) AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) AS snapshot_date,
        src."v1.issue.package" AS issue_package,
        src."v1.issue.language" AS issue_language,
        -- cast CVSS score to numeric when it is a numeric-looking string
        CASE
            WHEN src."v1.issue.cvssScore" ~ '^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' THEN CAST(src."v1.issue.cvssScore" AS NUMERIC)
            ELSE NULL
        END AS cvss_score,
        -- derive high CVSS flag
        CASE
            WHEN src."v1.issue.cvssScore" ~ '^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$' AND CAST(src."v1.issue.cvssScore" AS NUMERIC) >= 7.0 THEN TRUE
            ELSE FALSE
        END AS is_high_cvss,
        CAST(src."v1.issue.publicationTime" AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) AS publication_time,
        src."v1.issue.semver.vulnerable" AS vulnerable_semver_ranges
    FROM {{ source('silver', 'snyk_issues') }} AS src
    {% if is_incremental() %}
    WHERE src."connection_id" = '{{ var("connection_id") }}'
    {% else %}
    WHERE src."connection_id" = '{{ var("connection_id") }}'
    {% endif %}
)

SELECT
    sd.connection_id,
    sd.organization_id,
    sd.snapshot_date,
    sd.issue_package,
    sd.issue_language,
    sd.cvss_score,
    sd.is_high_cvss,
    sd.publication_time,
    sd.vulnerable_semver_ranges
FROM source_data AS sd
gold_model_sql.description: Daily snapshot of Snyk vulnerabilities (gold layer). Normalizes nested fields from the silver.snyk_issues source, casts CVSS to numeric, computes high-CVSS flag, and normalizes publication time to a daily snapshot_date. Incremental materialization filtered by connection_id.

gold_model_sql.name: gold_snyk_issues_weekly_snapshot
gold_model_sql.sql_query:
{{ config(materialized='incremental', unique_key=['connection_id','week_start','issue_language','issue_package'], incremental_strategy='merge', on_schema_change='append_new_columns') }}

select
  daily.connection_id,
  CAST(DATE_TRUNC('week', daily.snapshot_date) AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE) AS week_start,
  daily.issue_language,
  daily.issue_package,
  SUM(CASE WHEN daily.is_high_cvss = TRUE THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS count_high_cvss_week,
  json_build_object(
    'min', MIN(daily.cvss_score),
    'p25', percentile_disc(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY daily.cvss_score),
    'p50', percentile_disc(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY daily.cvss_score),
    'p75', percentile_disc(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY daily.cvss_score),
    'max', MAX(daily.cvss_score),
    'avg', AVG(daily.cvss_score)
  ) AS cvss_distribution_week
from {{ ref('gold_snyk_issues_daily_snapshot') }} as daily
where daily.connection_id = {{ var('connection_id') }}
{% if is_incremental() %}
  -- incremental runs only process recent snapshots (by snapshot_date -> week_start)
  AND daily.snapshot_date >= (
    select coalesce(max(week_start), '1970-01-01'::timestamp) from {{ this }}
  )
{% endif %}
group by
  daily.connection_id,
  CAST(DATE_TRUNC('week', daily.snapshot_date) AS TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE),
  daily.issue_language,
  daily.issue_package;
gold_model_sql.description: Weekly aggregation of Snyk issue snapshots (vulnerabilities). Computes week_start from daily snapshot_date, counts high-CVSS issues per week and returns a CVSS distribution (min, p25, p50, p75, max, avg) grouped by connection, language, and package.

candidate_templates.name: Vulnerability Posture
candidate_templates.description: Dedicated vuln management view. Patch SLA KPIs + CVE list with severity + detail with CISA KEV, CVSS, blast radius + remediation chat.

candidate_templates.name: Executive Risk Summary
candidate_templates.description: CISO/board-level single-page view. Wide KPI strip (8 cells) + 2-panel with risk heatmap + framework posture cards. No chat, no drill-down — pure signal.

candidate_templates.name: Access Review & Certification
candidate_templates.description: IAM-focused: access reviews, orphaned accounts, privilege creep. Links Workday terminations → Cornerstone certs → Security access data.

candidate_templates.name: Risk Register Dashboard
candidate_templates.description: GRC risk register view. Risk items with owner/status/score + detail with treatment plan + evidence + AI risk advisor.

candidate_templates.name: Third-Party / Vendor Risk
candidate_templates.description: Vendor risk management. Vendor list with risk tiers + assessment detail + SLA compliance + contract signals.

candidate_templates.description: Full 3-panel with posture strip. Master list → Detail → AI Chat. The canonical CCE layout.

candidate_templates.description: Wide KPI strip + 2-panel. No chat — pure monitoring and reporting view.

candidate_templates.description: Workday HR operations. Workforce KPIs + employee/position list + detail + AI advisor.

candidate_templates.name: Migration Tracker
candidate_templates.description: System migration tracking (SumTotal→Cornerstone etc). Progress KPIs + table mapping list + detail.

candidate_templates.name: Data Pipeline Health
candidate_templates.description: DS pipeline monitoring. DAG/job list with status + run detail + data quality signals + lineage graph.

candidate_templates.name: Onboarding / Offboarding Workflow
candidate_templates.description: Employee lifecycle spanning Workday (HR trigger) → Cornerstone (training enrollment) → Security (provisioning/deprovisioning). Tracks SLA at each handoff.

candidate_templates.name: Regulatory Change Tracker
candidate_templates.description: Tracks regulatory changes (HIPAA, SOC2, NIST AI RMF, etc.) and their impact on existing controls. Change list + impact assessment + gap analysis.
